# Installed mechanisms — `VIDEO`

<!-- The 'Wired hooks' and 'Catalogue coverage' sections are GENERATED by
     tools/report-installed.py from this machine's live config. Do not hand-edit them.
     Every other section is preserved verbatim across regenerations — put judgement,
     declined items and non-standard install notes there. -->

`mechanisms/` is a catalogue, not a runtime. This file is the record of what is actually
**in force on this machine** — so nobody has to infer it from the catalogue's contents.

## Wired hooks (read from the live config)

| Event | Matcher | Hook file | File present? | Config |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `PreToolUse` | `Bash` | `lying_command_guard.py` | yes | `settings.json` |
| `PreToolUse` | `Write|Edit|MultiEdit|NotebookEdit` | `repo_doc_guard.py` | yes | `settings.json` |
| `SessionStart` | `*` | `pending_instructions.py` | yes | `settings.json` |
| `Stop` | `*` | `evidence_with_claim.py` | yes | `settings.json` |
| `Stop` | `*` | `output_budget.py` | yes | `settings.json` |
| `Stop` | `*` | `requirement_before_mechanism.py` | yes | `settings.json` |
| `Stop` | `*` | `workflow_output_to_repo.py` | yes | `settings.json` |

## Catalogue coverage

Which catalogue entries are wired here. **Not-wired is a legitimate choice** — record
the reason in a section below, since this table can only report the fact, not the why.

| Catalogue entry | Kind | Wired to an event here? |
|---|---|---|
| `mechanisms/hooks/evidence_with_claim.py` | event hook | yes |
| `mechanisms/hooks/evidence_with_claim_test.py` | test | n/a |
| `mechanisms/hooks/hook_log.py` | library/CLI | n/a |
| `mechanisms/hooks/hook_rollup.py` | library/CLI | n/a |
| `mechanisms/hooks/pending_instructions.py` | event hook | yes |

## Declined, and why

- **Nothing declined yet.** VIDEO is where most of these were authored, so it wires everything
  that is an event hook. A machine that adopts fewer should say so here rather than leaving the
  coverage table to imply neglect — *not adopted* and *not yet looked at* are different states
  and the generated table cannot tell them apart.

## Hooks wired here but NOT yet in the catalogue

Wired on this box, not yet contributed to `mechanisms/` — listed so the gap is visible rather
than implied by absence:

- `lying_command_guard.py` (PreToolUse/Bash) — rejects command shapes that return a plausible
  WRONG answer (`cd X && git …` reporting on the wrong repo, a filtered context reading, a
  detached pacer). Each pattern is one that produced a confident wrong conclusion in a real run.
- `repo_doc_guard.py` (PreToolUse/edit tools) — orientation gate: read a repo's CLAUDE.md before
  editing in it. **Known defect, do not copy blind:** it keys on the SESSION transcript, not the
  acting agent, so a subagent can never satisfy it (false DENY) and a subagent that never read is
  admitted because the parent did (false ALLOW — the dangerous half).
- `output_budget.py` (Stop) — per-turn chat character budget.
- `requirement_before_mechanism.py` (Stop) — a turn that edits source must state the requirement.
- `workflow_output_to_repo.py` (Stop) — a turn that ran a Workflow must write into a repo.

## Non-standard locations

- Hook scripts: `~/.claude/hooks/` (standard). Wiring: `~/.claude/settings.json`.
- Hook fire log: `~/.claude/hook-events.jsonl`, read by `hook_rollup.py`. Append-only; it records
  that a hook fired, never a verdict on whether it was right.
- Loose mechanisms at `~/.claude/` top level: `turn-pacer.py`, `context-usage.py`, `gotcha.py`,
  `claim-check.py`, `mine-transcript.py`, `statusline-context.py`.
- Per-host config backup: `~/dotfiles-bs` (**not** under `Documents/GitHub`), pushed every ~20 min.
  It is a *safety net for this machine*, invisible to the other one — cross-machine sharing is
  this repo, which is the distinction that let seven of those loose scripts go unbacked-up for
  weeks without anyone noticing.
